title: composition.v1
description: The STRUCTURED generation contract the AI emits (brand-schema.md §4.6). The AI does NOT author raw HTML/CSS; it emits an ordered list of SECTIONS, each = archetype + slots (primitive/block refs) + treatments + inline copy. A deterministic renderer (compose_page/compose_section + component_render) draws it and the on-brand gate (onbrand_check.py) validates it. Every field round-trips 1:1 onto an existing brand.yaml layouts[]-shaped dict + renderer input, so the AI gets layout freedom WITHOUT re-authoring primitives or bypassing the gate. Vocabulary is reused verbatim from layout-patterns.v1 (§4.4) so the round-trip is lossless.

brief.description: What the page is for. Drives SELECT + ORDER + BIND. Copy the AI binds into slots comes from here (never hardcoded).

text.description: raw brief copy the AI drew inline copy from

useCasesRequested.description: the use-cases the brief asked for, in intent order

brand.description: Which brand.yaml this composition targets. The renderer resolves tokens/neverDo/type-tiers from it; the composition only references token ROLES, never raw values.

ref.description: path to the canonical brand.yaml, e.g. runs/woodwave/brand/brand.yaml

style.description: The active STYLE layer (styles/<id>.md) merged under the brand (Appendix B). Structure/defaults only; brand wins on any token it sets.

sections.description: Ordered list of sections = the page. Order is authored by the AI (SELECT + ORDER) and consumed by compose_page in list order (replaces the hardcoded DEFAULT_ORDER).

rationale.description: Free-text: why these sections/this order/these novel departures. Advisory; not rendered.

archetype.description: The structural shape. MUST be a key in compose_section.ARCHETYPE_COMPOSERS so the renderer can draw it. The eight drawable archetypes: 'stack', 'collage', 'split', 'stack-fullbleed', 'cards', 'interlock', plus (editorial-harvest-2026-07) 'overlay' — front slots grid-placed over a z:back/mid media inside ONE positioning context (panel-on-media, framed media, scrim bands, straddles, type-behind-media) — and 'banded' — a dual-surface section: two stacked full-width surface bands with a hard horizontal SEAM at section.bands.split, elements may straddle the seam via registration.toSlot:'seam'. (An AI may still set novelty:'novel' and recompose slots/treatments WITHIN one of these archetypes; it may not invent an archetype the renderer cannot draw.)

surfaceIntent.description: Which surface role this section defaults to (§4.4 surfaceIntent). Canonical intents (any, primary, panel, inverse, inverse-strong) resolve for every brand; ANY OTHER value must be the suffix of one of THIS brand's own tokens.surfaces roles (surface/<value>, e.g. a brand-declared 'raised' or 'accent-wash') — the renderer honors it only when the brand declares that role and keeps the historical degrade otherwise (fact-gated; never an invented palette). Surface choice is part of the copy-first layout plan (spec/archetype-library.md §3).

novelty.description: How far this section departs from the seeded library pattern. reuse = keep the pattern's shape (fill slots + tune knobs only); adapt = nearest pattern + tuned variantKnobs / minor slot changes; novel = a structure the library lacks (still must validate against this schema + brand neverDo; if it gates green it is eligible for promotion into the project layout-library).

seededFrom.description: Back-ref to the layout PATTERN this section was seeded from (§4.4 patternRef). null when novelty=novel and no pattern was used.

textLen.description: Text-length class (§4.4). short≈eyebrow(≤6w) · long≈body(>40w). Resolves to a type tier at render time; never a px size.

sizeClass.description: Type-size tier class (§4.4). Resolves against the brand's measured type tiers + style display floor; never a raw rem.

width.description: Harvest width class (§4.4). Resolves against the container measure; never a raw width. 'framed' (editorial-harvest-2026-07, G4): media inset with page margins visible on ALL sides (between 'media' and 'full-bleed'); the frame margin snaps to whole shared-grid columns and child slots may register INSIDE the frame via alignTo {slot: <frame slot>, edge|corner}.

mediaAspect.description: Media aspect class for media slots (§4.4). Honored by the renderer as a real aspect-ratio: portrait 3/4 · landscape (variant default) · square 1/1 · wide 21/9 · pano 3/1 · freeform (intrinsic).

grid.description: OPTIONAL section-level grid declaration (grid/overlap contract). Declares the registration grid this section's colStart/colSpan/offsetCols values are expressed against. Omitted = the shared page grid (12 columns, the :root --grid-gutter). columns/gutter re-scope the section's --grid-cols/--grid-gutter vars only — the page-level shared grid is unchanged.

columns.description: column count of the section's registration grid (default 12 = the shared page grid)

gutter.description: gutter as a rem/named length (never px-literal ad-hoc); omitted = the shared --grid-gutter

alignment.description: OPTIONAL per-section alignment knob. Replaces the old #sec-0-only centering exception: ANY section may declare its anchor. 'centered' mirrors the sanctioned hero/cta centering; 'left'/'right' anchor the text column to one side — an asymmetric anchor SHOULD name a counterweight slot (media/ghost/panel) that fills the opposite side so the section never reads as crammed against a dead half.

counterweight.description: slot NAME expected to visually balance an asymmetric anchor (advisory; the renderer stamps it for review, the model must supply a real slot)

edge.description: An edge of a slot's rendered box.

corner.description: A corner of a slot's (or the section's) rendered box: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right.

alignTo.description: OPTIONAL slot-level alignment reference: pin THIS slot to another slot's edge or corner (or to the SECTION frame when slot is omitted/'section'). Non-overlapping registration — for overlaps that CROSS an edge use `registration` instead.

slot.description: target slot NAME ('section' or omitted = the section frame)

registration.description: OPTIONAL overlap registration (replaces magic % offsets): THIS slot overlaps `toSlot`, pinned to and CROSSING its `edge` by an explicit depth in shared grid units — depthCols (columns+gutters, horizontal edges) or depthBaselines (--baseline multiples, vertical edges). `z` declares the layer relative to the base media (back = behind it, mid = between media, front = topmost media layer; text stays above per the brand z-ladder).

toSlot.description: slot NAME of the base element this overlay registers to

depthCols.description: how far the overlay crosses the edge, in whole/fractional shared-grid columns (horizontal edges)

depthBaselines.description: how far the overlay crosses the edge, in --baseline multiples (vertical edges)

z.description: Z-layer (§4.4). Must respect brand z-order (ghost-watermark → media → panels → text).

amountClass.description: Treatment intensity class (§4.4 amount.class). Never a px offset.

slot.description: One filled hole in the section. name/role/contract are required; the rest are the layout-patterns.v1 slot signature (§4.4) plus optional bound copy/asset. contract MUST be a key in contracts/primitives.yaml or contracts/blocks.yaml (respect the block's accepts/optional/repeatable grammar).

name.description: slot name, e.g. heading, media, body

role.description: semantic role, e.g. display-title, hero-photo, lede

contract.description: primitive OR block contract key (contracts/primitives.yaml | contracts/blocks.yaml), e.g. header, image, link, paragraph

colStart.description: OPTIONAL 1-based start column on the section's registration grid (grid-column start)

colSpan.description: OPTIONAL column span on the section's registration grid; the renderer resolves it to calc(N*var(--col) + (N-1)*var(--grid-gutter)) so the slot's edges land on shared column lines

rowSpan.description: OPTIONAL row span (multi-row scaffolds); advisory where an archetype has no explicit rows

offsetCols.description: OPTIONAL horizontal nudge in shared-grid columns (may be fractional/negative); a deliberate registered offset, never a free % scalar

offsetBaselines.description: OPTIONAL vertical nudge in --baseline multiples (may be fractional/negative)

copy.description: Inline copy bound from the brief. String for a single text slot, or an object for multi-field blocks (e.g. {eyebrow, heading, subheading, cta}). Repeatable blocks (block.accepts.repeatable) carry an array.

asset.description: Bound media asset for media slots. null when the renderer should use a placeholder.

treatment.description: A signature composition device. Reuses the layout-patterns.v1 specialTreatments shape verbatim (§4.4) so it round-trips into gridRules/overlapRules. kind-specific fields are used per kind; unused fields omitted. 'sanctioned' marks a device that is only legal because a brand compositionRule/neverDo blesses it (e.g. WoodWave display-title-over-media).

kind.description: Device kind (layout_library.TREATMENT_KINDS). 'text-on-media' is illegal for WoodWave EXCEPT the sanctioned display-title-over-media in a hero bookend (mark sanctioned:true). editorial-harvest-2026-07 additions: 'straddle' = target crosses another slot's edge by registration.depth (z:front rides over, z:back tucks UNDER — a z:back straddle carries the G8 occlusion contract maxOcclusion+endsVisible and, when it meets a photo, sanctioned:true); 'panel-on-media' = a solid-surface panel (target) grid-placed over a media slot ('over'), internal stack distributed by 'distribute' — the sanctioned panel-over-media pair; 'scrim-band' = a flat translucent band (NEVER a gradient) crossing the media at band.rowStart/rowSpan carrying the target slot's content; 'framed' = the target media renders at width:framed (margins on all sides, widthRel.ratio of the container snapped to whole columns); 'type-behind-media' = the target HEADING renders z:back with the 'over' media on top — real copy at full opacity (NOT a ghost-word); requires maxOcclusion + endsVisible (G8) and counts as a text-on-media-family device (sanctioned where the brand requires it); 'mixed-face' = per-span face contrast inside one heading (copy carries {lead, emphasis}; falls back to case/weight contrast when the brand has no italic cut); 'stepped-lines' = per-line progressive indents on an authored multi-line statement (steps in half-column units); 'break-frame' = corner-anchored decorative media crossing a frame edge (salience-capped, decoration only).

target.description: the slot this device applies to (ghost-word/stagger/bleed/marginal-caption/text-on-media)

zOrder.description: layer order back→front for overlap/ghost-word (must respect brand z-order ladder)

over.description: media slot a text-on-media title sits over (sanctioned hero exception only)

registration.description: straddle/panel-on-media/break-frame: the overlap registration this device rides on (toSlot + crossed edge + depth in shared grid units + z). For a straddle inside a 'banded' section, toSlot may be the reserved name 'seam' (the band boundary).

band.description: scrim-band geometry: where the band crosses the media, as fractions of the media's height (rowStart 0..1, rowSpan 0..1). Snapped to --baseline at render time.

fill.description: scrim-band fill: a FLAT translucent wash of the section's inverse surface (never a gradient). opacityClass light≈0.35 / medium≈0.55 / heavy≈0.75 — the floor is derived from the text-contrast requirement.

distribute.description: panel-on-media: how the panel's internal stack distributes vertically

widthRel.description: framed/inset: target width as a ratio of the container (snapped to whole shared-grid columns), e.g. {to: container, ratio: 0.86}

maxOcclusion.description: G8 occlusion budget for type-behind-media AND a z:back straddle: the fraction of the occluded heading's glyph area media may cover — light≈0.25 / medium≈0.4 / heavy≈0.55. Above the cap the gate demands reclassification to ghost-word (decorative + readable heading elsewhere).

endsVisible.description: G8: the occluded word's FIRST and LAST letterforms must stay clear of the media (why an occluded masthead still reads). Required true for type-behind-media / z:back straddle on real heading copy.

steps.description: stepped-lines: per-line left indents in HALF-COLUMN units of the shared grid (e.g. [0, 1, 6] = 0 / +0.5col / +3col)

direction.description: stepped-lines: which way successive lines step

spans.description: mixed-face: which copy part renders in which face (copy carries {lead, emphasis}); degrades to case/weight contrast when the brand ships no alternate cut

salience.description: break-frame: the element is decoration — area-capped, pointer-events none, must never cover a text slot (decoration-salience applies)

sanctioned.description: true when a normally-forbidden device is blessed by a brand compositionRule/neverDo exception (e.g. WoodWave sanctioned display-title-over-media). false/absent = must be a legal device.

bands.description: OPTIONAL dual-surface declaration for the 'banded' archetype (G9, editorial-harvest-2026-07): the section is TWO stacked full-width surface bands with a hard horizontal SEAM (a hard cut, never a gradient). 'split' is the seam's position as a fraction of section height (snapped to --baseline); 'surfaces' names the top and bottom band surface roles. Slots may straddle the seam via registration {toSlot: 'seam', edge: top|bottom, depthBaselines}. Content is surface-attributed to the band it sits on; straddlers to both. This is a SINGLE section — cross-SECTION straddling is deliberately unsupported.

id.description: section slug, unique within the composition, e.g. hero, features, cta

archetypeRef.description: OPTIONAL genre-archetype id from contracts/archetypes/*.yaml (spec/archetype-library.md) — the structural skeleton this section instantiates. The renderer normalizes the section against the archetype BEFORE brand adaptation (brand recipes win), and demotes to the brand's own evidenced anatomy when the id is unknown or a physics fact family cannot bind (fail closed). Only offered by the generator when hero structure candidates were injected.

knobs.description: variantKnob values the AI tuned (§4.4 variantKnobs), e.g. {mediaSide: left, columns: '3', align: left}. Round-trips into the renderer's --c-* vars / archetype options. Only knobs the seeded pattern (or archetype) exposes.
